era: 1989-1994. Alternative Rock. E=6 -- driving urgency, riff-forward, Carrie Akre voice. C/Z to A&M.

theory_nodes.concept: Female vocalist standout in male-dominated grunge era. Akre's voice: powerful, emotionally direct, Kate Bush range. Defense of space.
theory_nodes.description: Carrie Akre is one of Seattle's undervalued vocal contributions.

theory_nodes.concept: C/Z Records enters engine at 222. Co-founded by Chris Hanzsek (Deep Six producer) and Daniel House (Skin Yard). Pre-Sub Pop infrastructure.
theory_nodes.description: C/Z as essential Seattle indie alongside Sub Pop and PopLlama.

theory_nodes.concept: 1989-1994 arc precisely brackets grunge underground-to-major-label window. Form → C/Z → A&M → dissolution matches Cobain's death.

cross_references.relationship: E=5 to E=6. Psychedelic to Alternative Rock. Sub Pop to C/Z/A&M. Energy rises.
E=6 holds. Alternative to Post-Grunge. Female vocal to male vocal. Same energy, different era.

sps_pair.pairing_note: Territorial call louder, more aggressive than 221. Long series of piercing calls = defense of space. Akre's voice in male-dominated scene = territorial claim. E=6 match.
